title: Bank of America Company Overview
text: Bank of America Corporation is one of the world's largest financial institutions. The company serves individual consumers, small and middle-market businesses, large corporations, governments, institutions, and investors. Its main business areas include Consumer Banking, Global Wealth and Investment Management, Global Banking, and Global Markets. Bank of America provides checking and savings products, credit cards, mortgages, auto loans, commercial lending, treasury services, wealth management, investment banking, trading, asset management, and financial risk management products. The company has a large U.S. retail banking network and a major digital banking platform, with tens of millions of verified digital users. For investment analysis, Bank of America is important because its earnings are influenced by interest rates, deposit balances, loan growth, credit quality, consumer spending, wealth management activity, investment banking, and trading revenue.
